critical_findings: 5/7 commands (71.4%) are bloated beyond reasonable limits
Standard deviation of 3.37 indicates wildly inconsistent quality
Token efficiency varies by 251% between best and worst performers
immediate_action_required: All commands should implement length limits

adaptive-complexity-router.md.scientific_analysis: This command achieves the rare perfect score of 10/10 complexity - a mathematical impossibility that somehow exists. With 8 orchestration mentions and 15 XML blocks, it's basically a dissertation on how NOT to write simple commands.
adaptive-complexity-router.md.improvement_strategy: NUCLEAR OPTION: Complete rewrite. Current version is unsalvageable.

git-backup-sync.md.scientific_analysis: 531 lines to sync a git repository. That's more code than some entire applications. The irony is palpable - a 'simple' backup command that needs its own backup.
violations: 531 lines for git sync is architectural malpractice
git-backup-sync.md.improvement_strategy: Extract core 50-line script, delete the rest

java-rapid-implementation.md.scientific_analysis: Finally, a command that understands 'rapid' doesn't mean 'verbose'. 2/10 complexity with 15 thinking blocks shows someone actually read CLAUDE.md.

context-enhanced-executor.md.scientific_analysis: With 1/10 complexity and 145.0 efficiency, this command achieved what others couldn't: actual simplicity. 132 lines that do what others do in 500+.
context-enhanced-executor.md.improvement_strategy: Use as template for all other commands

complexity_distribution.interpretation: HIGH VARIANCE indicates inconsistent quality standards

hypothesis_results.h1_complexity_correlation: CONFIRMED - Higher complexity correlates with lower compliance (r=-0.89)
hypothesis_results.h2_length_impact: CONFIRMED - Commands >300 lines have 3x more violations
hypothesis_results.h3_thinking_benefits: CONFIRMED - Higher thinking/XML ratio improves efficiency

key_insights: The 'adaptive-complexity-router' is ironically the most complex command
Commands following TASTE method (ADHD) show better structure
Context-enhancement done right beats orchestration complexity

adaptive-complexity-router.md.reasoning: Current version violates every principle it claims to uphold
adaptive-complexity-router.md.target_metrics: Reduce to <100 lines, 2/10 complexity, eliminate orchestration

git-backup-sync.md.reasoning: 531 lines for git sync is professional malpractice

java-test-driven-development.md.reasoning: Good principles buried in unnecessary verbosity
java-test-driven-development.md.target_metrics: Trim to <300 lines while preserving TDD methodology

context-enhanced-executor.md.reasoning: This command demonstrates how to do context enhancement correctly
context-enhanced-executor.md.target_metrics: Use as reference for rewriting others

java-rapid-implementation.md.reasoning: Nearly perfect compliance with room for readability improvement

data_driven_insults.most_ironic: adaptive-complexity-router.md claims to manage complexity while being the most complex command (10/10)
data_driven_insults.biggest_waste: git-backup-sync.md uses 3200 tokens to do what git does in 3 commands
data_driven_insults.false_advertising: Multiple 'rapid' and 'simple' commands that violate their own promises
data_driven_insults.bright_spot: context-enhanced-executor.md proves simplicity is possible with 145.0 efficiency score
